symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread pruritic red-brown maculopapular lesions that wheal/swell on friction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urticaria pigmentosa lesions that wheal on friction are highly characteristic of cutaneous/systemic mastocytosis.
Generic urticaria can look similar but does not explain the pigmented, friction-provoked pattern or bone pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep throbbing bone pain in both femurs, concurrent with the flare

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse long-bone pain reflects marrow mast cell infiltrate — a feature of systemic mastocytosis.
Diffuse pain tied to the acute degranulation timeline, non-focal, argues against destructive focal metastatic lesions.
Flushing is a cardinal mast cell mediator (histamine/prostaglandin) symptom.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known clonal mast cell disorder with prior recurrent anaphylaxis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established prior diagnosis anchors the current flare and mandates anaphylaxis safety-netting.
Prior recurrent anaphylaxis raises the standing baseline risk that a flare could progress.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent airway/circulatory/GI involvement (no throat swelling, no wheeze, no presyncope, no vomiting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The negative multi-system screen is the pivotal evidence that this is a mediator flare and NOT anaphylaxis — it is what keeps triage below T5.

symptoms.symptom_name: Darier's sign positive (lesions wheal/swell when stroked)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Darier's sign is near-pathognomonic for cutaneous mastocytosis — but requires clinician examination, so it is sealed and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normotensive with patent airway (BP 115/75, SpO2 99%, airway clear, no stridor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved blood pressure, oxygenation and a patent airway on clinician measurement exclude anaphylactic shock/airway compromise. Sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers stability from the patient-reported negative screen, not a measured BP.
Mild tachycardia is explained by mediator release, distress and pruritus; non-specific and, in isolation with a normal BP, not a marker of shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum mast cell tryptase (baseline and acute)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A persistently elevated baseline tryptase supports systemic mastocytosis; an acute rise supports mediator release. Laboratory investigation — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Cutaneous + skeletal + flushing mediator symptoms WITH a negative multi-system anaphylaxis screen defines a stable flare — the pattern that fixes triage below T5.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the systemic mastocytosis diagnosis and quantifies mediator burden; arranged via GP/immunology, not an emergency test.
Baseline haematological assessment for systemic disease; outpatient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department bloods / cardiac work-up for presumed anaphylaxis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-investigation: with a negative red-flag screen and stable haemodynamics this is a mediator flare, not an emergency — routing to ED bloods is over-triage.
